summary: Belgian painter known for masks, skeletons, and grotesque imagery. Most commonly identified by Christ's Entry Into Brussels in 1889 (now at the Getty) and Skeletons Fighting Over a Pickled Herring. Member of Les XX (Les Vingt).

works.name: Christ's Entry Into Brussels in 1889
works.description: Monumental painting showing a procession of mask wearers under a banner reading 'Vive La Sociale' with Christ on a donkey. Kept rolled up for 30 years; now at the Getty Museum. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Procession of mask wearers under a banner reading 'Vive La Sociale'; Christ enters on a donkey
examples: A procession of mask wearers largely ignore the title figure underneath a banner reading "Vive La Sociale"
skeletons under a banner reading "Vive la Sociale" in Christ's Entry into Brussels

examples: This artist wears a red feathered hat in a crowded canvas depicting himself surrounded by people wearing masks

clues.clue: Roundel depicting crucifixion above figures with bloody hands; frogs jump from black cauldron at bottom left
examples: a roundel depicting the crucifixion hangs above figures with bloody hands. At the bottom left, frogs jump out of a black cauldron

works.name: Skeletons Fighting Over a Pickled Herring
works.description: Two skeletons play tug-of-war over a pickled herring; Ensor represents himself as the herring, mocking his unfriendly critics.

two skulls playing tug-of-war over a pickled herring with their teeth

comprehensive_summary:
James Ensor was a Belgian painter known for grotesque imagery featuring masks and skeletons. His masterpiece, Christ's Entry Into Brussels in 1889, shows a procession of mask wearers under a banner reading 'Vive La Sociale' largely ignoring Christ on a donkey. It was kept rolled up for 30 years and is now at the Getty Museum. A roundel depicting the crucifixion hangs above figures with bloody hands, and frogs jump from a black cauldron. Ensor wears a red feathered hat in the crowd.

Skeletons Fighting Over a Pickled Herring shows two skeletons in a tug-of-war over a herring; Ensor represents himself as the fish, mocking his critics. He was a member of Les XX (Les Vingt), formed partly due to rejection of his Oyster Eaters by the L'Essor group. His mother Marie owned a curio shop whose oriental vases and dolls appear in his paintings. Other works include The Dangerous Cooks (depicting Octave Maus and Edmond Picard) and The Seven Deadly Sins Dominated by Death. He painted a self-portrait imagining himself at age 100, menaced by a spider.
